metadata.title: my-api — Custom REST Endpoints
metadata.description: Define your own data schemas, generate REST endpoints, and query them from anywhere with per-account access tokens.

common.name: Name
common.somethingWentWrongRetry: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.eyebrow: Your data → your REST API, in minutes
hero.title: Build custom REST endpoints without writing a backend
hero.description: Define your own data structures, generate secure REST endpoints from them, and call them from anywhere with access tokens that are unique to your account.
hero.code:
curl -X POST https://your-app/api/v1/notes \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer mapi_•••••••••" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "title": "Hello", "done": false }'

define.title: Define schemas
define.body: Describe your data once — fields, types, required rules. No migrations, no boilerplate.

generate.title: Generate REST endpoints
generate.body: Turn any schema into a real REST resource. Pick the verbs, choose what's readable and writable.

query.title: Query with access tokens
query.body: Mint per-account tokens scoped to specific endpoints. Revoke any time. Strictly isolated per user.

one.title: Create a schema
one.description: e.g. a Note with title, body, done.

three.title: Issue a token
three.description: Scoped to that endpoint, read + write.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.description: Start free and upgrade when you need more endpoints, tokens, and throughput.

hobby.name: Hobby
hobby.tagline: For side projects and trying things out.

pro.name: Pro
pro.tagline: For production apps that need room to grow.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.tagline: For teams with scale, security, and SLA needs.

landing.footer: my-api — custom REST endpoints backed by MongoDB, Redis, and per-account access tokens.

signIn.title: Welcome back
signIn.description: Sign in to manage your endpoints.

signUp.title: Create your account
signUp.description: Start building custom REST endpoints in minutes.

schemas.title: Schemas
schemas.description: Define the shape of your data. Endpoints are built on top of schemas.
schemas.emptyDescription: Create your first schema to describe the data your endpoints will store.
schemas.deleteConfirm: Delete schema "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

modal.description: Describe a data type with typed fields.
modal.editWarning: Editing fields won't change records already stored. Removing a field just hides it from future reads and writes.

endpoints.title: Endpoints
endpoints.description: Expose schemas as REST endpoints. Choose verbs and what can be read or written.
endpoints.emptyNeedsSchemaDescription: Endpoints are built on schemas. Head to the Schemas tab to define one.
endpoints.emptyDescription: Turn one of your schemas into a callable REST endpoint.
endpoints.deleteConfirm: Delete endpoint "{{name}}"? Its stored records will be removed.

modal.description: Expose a schema as a REST resource.
modal.readableHint: Returned by GET and GET many
modal.readableRequired: Select at least one readable field, or turn off the GET methods.
modal.writableRequired: Select at least one writable field, or turn off the write methods.

tokens.title: Request Tokens
tokens.description: Request tokens authenticate external calls to your endpoints. Each is scoped to the endpoints and permissions you choose.
tokens.emptyNeedsEndpointDescription: Request tokens grant access to specific endpoints. Create one in the Endpoints tab.
tokens.emptyDescription: Mint a request token to start calling your endpoints from anywhere.
tokens.deleteConfirm: Delete token "{{name}}"? Calls using it will stop working.

modal.createdDescription: Copy this token now, or view it again anytime from the token list below.
modal.bearerHint: Use it as a bearer token:
modal.newDescription: Choose which endpoints this request token can reach and what it can do.
modal.editDescription: Update the name and which endpoints this request token can reach. The token value stays the same.
modal.name: Request token name
modal.grantRequired: Grant access to at least one endpoint

entries.title: Entries
entries.description: Browse and edit the data stored in each of your schemas.
entries.emptySchemasDescription: Entries live inside schemas. Create a schema first, then manage its data here.

editor.description: Changes are staged locally until you save them.
editor.importDisabledTitle: Save or discard your changes before importing
editor.emptyDescription: Add entries by hand or import a JSON file that matches this schema.
editor.nothingSaved: Nothing was saved — fix the highlighted entries and try again
editor.invalidJson: That file is not valid JSON
editor.expectedArray: Expected a JSON array of entries

importResult.warning: The rejected entries are only available right now — once you close this dialog they cannot be retrieved. Download them to fix and re-import later.

unsaved.title: Unsaved changes
unsaved.description: You have unsaved changes in this schema's entries.
unsaved.body: If you leave now, your staged edits will be discarded. Save your changes first to keep them.

errors.schemaSlugExists: You already have a schema with that slug
errors.schemaInUse: Schema is used by {{count}} endpoint(s). Delete those endpoints first.
errors.endpointFieldsInvalid: Readable/writable fields must belong to the schema
errors.endpointSlugExists: You already have an endpoint with that slug
errors.tokenRevealUnsupported: This token was created before viewing was supported — revoke it and create a new one
errors.endpointsUnknown: Some endpoints are unknown or not yours
errors.tokenEndpointForbidden: This token is not authorized for this endpoint
errors.methodDisabled: {{method}} is not enabled for this endpoint
errors.duplicateUpdateId: duplicate record id in update batch
errors.entriesInvalid: Some entries are invalid — nothing was saved
errors.duplicateBatch: duplicate value for unique field "{{field}}" — an earlier entry in the file already uses it
errors.duplicateStored: value for unique field "{{field}}" already exists in your stored entries

validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters
validation.fieldName: Use letters, numbers, underscores; must not start with a number
validation.enumValuesMin: Add at least one enum value
validation.tokenGrantsMin: Grant access to at least one endpoint
validation.tooManyChanges: Too many changes in one save (max 2000)
validation.tooManyImport: Too many entries in one import (max 5000)

record.bodyObject: Body must be a JSON object
record.manyBody: Body must be an array of records, or an object with an updates/records array
